bull.b: Multi-decade, triple-net style contracts and ~$16.8B of contracted revenue that convert to roughly $1.1B annual NOI materially shift company cash flows from mining cyclicality toward predictable, long-duration infrastructure income, supporting financing, capital recycling and planning over years.

A lengthy, nonrecourse financing with investment-grade ratings validates the capital‑efficient project funding model: it reduces parent refinancing risk, enables high loan-to-cost leverage for greenfield sites, and creates repeatable financing capacity to scale without materially increasing corporate recourse.

An 8.4GW pipeline and proven delivery of multiple greenfield AI campuses indicate scalable execution capability and multi-year growth runway in high‑demand compute infrastructure, allowing the company to monetize power-first sites via leases and hosting as demand for AI/data center capacity expands.

bear.b: Persistent negative operating and free cash flow means the business consumes cash despite revenue growth, forcing reliance on external financing or asset sales to fund capex and operations; this constrains financial flexibility and increases vulnerability to capital‑market or funding shocks.

Large unrealized mark‑to‑market swings on Bitcoin holdings create non‑cash earnings volatility that obscures operating performance, complicates forecasting and covenant assessment, and ties headline profitability to BTC price moves rather than core infrastructure economics.

bear.t: Execution & Timing Risk On Greenfield Projects
bear.b: Long multi‑year build schedules expose realized NOI to delays, cost overruns and regulatory hurdles; because material infrastructure revenue only ramps when sites come online, any slippage defers contracted cash flows and strains liquidity and capital recycling assumptions.
